mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 4.49% represents a restrictive monetary stance. Sustained elevation of FEDFUNDS above 4.0% signals ongoing credit tightening that reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates and compressed net present value of future corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.7% combined with month-over-month payroll growth deceleration signals labor market softening. When PAYEMS growth falls below 50,000 persons per month while UNRATE rises, equity investors anticipate earnings pressure from reduced consumer spending, triggering negative 5-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138,467 thousand, UNRATE at 4.7% as of November 2007
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of PAYEMS and UNRATE shows no statistically significant interaction (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of forward return sign.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) at 385,753 million dollars reflect consumer spending momentum. A month-over-month decline in RSAFS of more than 1.5% signals demand destruction that cascades into earnings downgrades and equity price compression over the subsequent 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 385,753 million dollars as of November 2007
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances following RSAFS declines exceeding -1.5%, or Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS growth and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) at 113.9 reflects manufacturing capacity utilization. Sustained month-over-month INDPRO growth above 0.3% signals robust supply-side momentum and pricing power that supports equity valuations, producing positive 20-day forward returns independent of demand conditions.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of periods with INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.3%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1,229 thousand units and real GDP growth (GDPC1) jointly determine the strength of the demand cycle. When HOUST falls below 1,200 thousand units while GDPC1 growth decelerates below 2.0% annualized, the economy enters a demand-constrained regime where equity multiples compress, producing negative 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,229 thousand units, GDPC1 at 11,659.3 billion as of October 2007
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between HOUST and GDPC1 growth is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in predicting forward return direction.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 210.565 combined with elevated FEDFUNDS at 4.49% signals the Fed is in a restrictive stance relative to inflation. When year-over-year CPI growth exceeds 3.0% while FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0%, real rates are sufficiently restrictive to suppress equity valuations, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 210.565, FEDFUNDS at 4.49% as of November 2007
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 53% of triggered instances, or the joint effect shows no significant interaction (p > 0.10) in explaining forward return variance beyond individual series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 138,467 thousand represents the stock of labor income generation. Sustained month-over-month PAYEMS growth above 100,000 persons signals robust labor income that supports consumer spending and equity earnings, producing positive 5-day forward returns in all macro regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138,467 thousand as of November 2007
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 51% of periods with PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 100,000, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1,229 thousand units represent forward-looking demand expectations. A three-month rolling average decline in HOUST below 1,100 thousand units signals a demand shock that precedes earnings downgrades by 60 trading days, producing negative 60-day forward returns in the post-shock period.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,229 thousand units as of October 2007
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 52% of instances following HOUST 3-month average drops below 1,100, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
